note: The Unfair Contract Terms Act 1977 restricts exclusion of liability for negligence and imposes a reasonableness test on limitation clauses in the UK.
UCC § 2-719(2) invalidates a limited remedy that fails of its essential purpose, which can defeat an otherwise-enforceable cap in extreme cases.
Waivers of indirect/consequential damages are standard and generally enforceable, but are often carved back for confidentiality breaches or indemnified claims.
Most jurisdictions will not enforce a liability cap as applied to a party's own gross negligence or willful misconduct even if the clause purports to cover it.
UK Unfair Contract Terms Act 1977 prohibits exclusion of liability for death/personal injury from negligence, and subjects other caps to reasonableness.
China Civil Code Article 506 voids exclusions for personal injury or property damage caused by intentional misconduct or gross negligence.
Australian Consumer Law voids any exclusion of statutory non-excludable consumer guarantees.
